notes: IBM is a major enterprise technology company with products spanning cloud, AI, middleware, databases, and systems. IBM communicates vulnerabilities through security bulletins, X-Force Exchange threat intelligence, and IBM Cloud security updates. IBM is a CVE Numbering Authority (CNA).

data.description: Product-specific security bulletins covering WebSphere, Db2, MQ, Cloud Paks, QRadar, and other IBM products.
data.notes: Searchable bulletin database. Query by product name, CVE, date, or severity. PDF downloads available. No formal ID pattern — bulletins use descriptive URLs.

data.description: Security bulletins for IBM Cloud services (IKS, ROKS, Cloud Foundry, Virtual Servers, managed databases).
IBM PSIRT coordinates vulnerability disclosure and response.
data.notes: Report vulnerabilities to psirt@us.ibm.com. Part of IBM Trust Center.

data.description: Threat intelligence platform providing vulnerability data, threat actor tracking, malware analysis, and IoCs.
data.notes: Free registration for access. API available. Broader than just IBM products. Vulnerability entries cross-reference CVEs.
